# host_sysctl (Resource)

Manages one Linux sysctl key.

This resource writes a provider-owned file under `/etc/sysctl.d` and applies the live value with `sysctl -w`. Destroying the resource removes the provider-owned file and leaves the live kernel value unchanged.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_sysctl" "ip_forward" {
  key   = "net.ipv4.ip_forward"
  value = "1"
}
```

## Import

Import an existing sysctl key by name:

```shell
terraform import host_sysctl.ip_forward net.ipv4.ip_forward
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `key` (String) Sysctl key, such as `vm.swappiness`.
- `value` (String) Desired sysctl value.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Resource identifier, equal to `key`.
- `path` (String) Provider-owned sysctl configuration file path.
